items.myth: High cross-validation accuracy proves there is no leakage.
items.reality: CV only protects against leakage if preprocessors and feature engineering are refit inside each fold. A single global fit on all data before CV still leaks across folds.
items.example: Computing TF-IDF on the full corpus once, then running 5-fold CV on the matrix, leaks document statistics between folds.

items.myth: Leakage and overfitting are the same problem fixed by regularization.
items.reality: Overfitting memorizes training noise with legitimate features; leakage imports invalid information (future data, label proxies). L2 penalty or dropout does not remove a column that encodes the answer.
items.example: Adding `will_refund` as a feature makes logistic regression 'perfect' on refunds; regularization will not delete the cheat column.

items.myth: If the test set is never used in `model.fit`, there is no leakage.
items.reality: Any step that learns from test rows—scaling, imputation, feature selection, deduplication, hyperparameter tuning on test—contaminates evaluation even if the estimator never saw test labels in `fit`.
items.example: Tuning k in KNN using test accuracy after scaling with global mean/std still leaks test distribution into inputs.
